titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 722

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=956 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 11:41:10.636317+00 | translation moa=957 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 11:41:10.626185+00

p.cl: Domino suo karissimo . D . comiti . Thor omnino suus . salutem .

Sciatis domine mi quod Eadgarus rex frater uester dedit
mihi Ednaham desertam . quam ego suo auxilio et mea pecunia inhabitaui . et ęcclesiam a fundamentis
fabricaui . quam frater uester rex in honorem Sancti Cuthberti fecit dedicari . et una carrucata terrę eam
dotauit . Hanc eandem ęcclesiam pro anima eiusdem domini mei regis Eadgari et patris et matris
uestrę . et pro salute uestra et regis Alexandri et Mathildis reginę Sancto prędicto et Monachis eius
dedi
Vnde uos precor sicut dominum meum karissimum . ut pro animabus parentum uestrorum et pro salute
uiuorum hanc donationem Sancto Cuthberto et Monachis sibi in perpetuum seruituris concedatis .
To his well-beloved lord, Earl David, Thor, entirely his, sends greeting.

Know, my lord, that King Edgar your brother gave me the wasteland of Ednam, which I have settled with his help and my own money, and I have built a church from the ground up, which your brother the king had dedicated in honour of Saint Cuthbert, and he endowed it with one ploughgate of land. This same church have I given to the aforesaid saint and his monks, for the soul of my same lord, King Edgar, and your father and mother, and for your own salvation, and the salvation of King Alexander and Queen Matilda. Wherefore I pray you as my well-beloved lord, that for the sake of the souls of your ancestors and for the salvation of the living, you approve this donation to Saint Cuthbert and in perpetuity to the monks who will serve him.
